title: How many players of Giannis Antetokounmpo’s caliber have been traded?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo, intrigued by joining the Celtics, could be a rare player of his caliber to be traded.
Only five players with as many All-NBA first-team selections as Antetokounmpo have been traded, including LeBron James and Kareem Abdul-Jabbar.
The Bucks plan to make a decision on Antetokounmpo's future before June 23, ahead of the NBA Draft.

title: Review & setlist: In Boston, St. Vincent goes symphonic to stunning effect
author: Mary Nordmann
bullets: St. Vincent performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on June 4, 2026, adding a symphonic element to her music.
Conductor Jules Buckley collaborated with St. Vincent, enhancing her songs with a 60-piece orchestra for a theatrical experience.
The concert featured tracks like 'Reckless' and 'Digital Witness,' concluding with encores 'Candy Darling' and 'Slow Disco.'

title: 5 storylines to follow as the Red Sox and Yankees battle in the Bronx
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Boston Red Sox face the New York Yankees in a three-game series starting Friday, with Connelly Early pitching Sunday.
Yankees' Ben Rice, hitting .300 with 17 home runs, seeks improvement against the Red Sox after a slow start this season.
Red Sox's Willson Contreras extends his on-base streak to 16 games, providing a key offensive boost for the team.

title: Canton issues statement after texts from former sergeant included in Karen Read’s new lawsuit
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Former Canton Police Sgt. Sean Goode resigned amid an internal affairs probe into derogatory text messages included in Karen Read's lawsuit.
Karen Read's lawsuit claims messages by Goode were vulgar and offensive, including slurs against public figures and derogatory comments about a murder victim.
Canton officials stated they took prompt action upon learning of the texts and committed to transparency in the investigation outcome.

title: ‘Old Thom,’ iconic solitary orca, spotted by Cape Cod fishermen
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Old Thom, a solitary orca with a unique dorsal fin notch, was spotted off the coast of Cape Cod.
Sureshot Charters posted a video of Old Thom while fishing for tuna near Chatham, Cape Cod this week.
Old Thom has been periodically seen off Cape Cod since 2015, with previous sightings near Stellwagen Bank.

title: Mass. House approves later bar hours, outdoor drinking zones for World Cup
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Massachusetts House approved a pilot program to extend bar hours to 3 a.m. and allow outdoor drinking zones.
The bill, H. 5471, applies from June 8 to July 31, coinciding with events like FIFA World Cup matches and Tall Ships.
The pilot program aims to benefit from a visitor influx, but there are concerns about late-night operations and worker conditions.

title: ‘It’s not done yet,’ Karen Read warns on ‘Today’ show after filing explosive new lawsuit
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Karen Read files lawsuit alleging law enforcement corruption after her acquittal in officer John O'Keefe's death.
Filed in Bristol Superior Court, the lawsuit accuses officers of bigotry and institutional biases in Massachusetts police.
Ex-Trooper Michael Proctor and ex-Sgt. Sean Goode implicated via slur-filled texts; Proctor fired, Goode resigned.

title: The best movies on Netflix right now (June 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix offers a curated list of movies ranging from new originals to classics like 'The Big Lebowski' and 'Creed.'
Drama offerings on Netflix include 'Atonement,' 'Ex Machina,' and 'His Three Daughters' featuring notable actors.
The list includes films from various genres, with updates every month to highlight new Netflix additions.

title: A guide to the New England ships in Sail Boston 2026
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will feature over 60 ships from more than 20 countries arriving on July 11.
The event is part of Sail250, celebrating the 250th anniversary of the United States with stops nationwide.
Attendees can tour participating ships and enjoy events like festivals and fireworks until July 16.

title: Large fire destroys multiple boats at Winthrop yacht club
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A large fire destroyed over 12 boats at Cottage Park Yacht Club in Winthrop on Friday morning.
Firefighters from multiple agencies responded; Massport, Boston, and Everett marine units were on scene.
The fire caused explosions; however, no injuries were reported despite significant damages to boats and docks.
